operating_state: Total sales and revenues increased 14 percent to $13.589 billion driven by higher sales volume and favorable price realization, partially offset by unfavorable currency impacts. Sales increased across all three primary segments with double-digit growth. Dealer inventories increased $1.3 billion during the quarter compared to $700 million in the prior year period, reflecting higher end-user demand amid broad-based recovery.
cost_structure: Operating profit margin compressed to 13.7 percent from 15.3 percent as unfavorable manufacturing costs and higher operating expenses more than offset favorable price realization and higher sales volume. Manufacturing costs increased primarily due to higher material and freight costs driven by supply chain pressures and component availability challenges. Short-term incentive compensation expense increased reflecting improved performance.
material_events: The company repurchased $820 million of common stock during the quarter under the 2018 Authorization, with $1.37 billion remaining available. Order backlog increased approximately $3.4 billion to $26.5 billion at quarter-end. Board director Miles D. White announced his decision not to stand for re-election at the 2022 Annual Meeting, and Chief Accounting Officer Gary Michael Marvel announced his retirement effective April 1, 2022.
macro_exposures: The company continues monitoring COVID-19 pandemic impacts, supply chain disruptions, and associated cost and labor pressures affecting components, transportation and raw materials. Transportation shortages have resulted in delays and increased costs, while supplier availability issues and freight delays create production pressure that limits production capacity despite strong demand recovery.
forward_outlook: Management expects price realization to more than offset manufacturing cost increases for 2022, with short-term incentive compensation expense expected to be approximately $1.3 billion. The company expects to incur restructuring costs primarily related to strategic actions, while prior restructuring actions will result in incremental benefit. Capital expenditures are expected to be about $1.5 billion for ME&T in 2022.
uncertainty_profile: The company does not expect a significant increase in dealer inventory in 2022 as conditions remain fluid. Cat Financial's allowance for credit losses increased to include a higher reserve for Russia and Ukraine portfolios, totaling $357 million or 1.29 percent of finance receivables. Contingency plans continue to be modified as supply chain challenges and external factors affecting end-market demand patterns evolve.
